title: NASW Code of Ethics Overview
summary: Professional ethics are at the core of social work and guide all decision-making on the ASWB exam.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the NASW Code of Ethics?
flashcards.back: To guide ethical decision-making and professional conduct.

title: NASW Code of Ethics Overview
summary: Professional ethics are at the core of social work and guide all decision-making on the ASWB exam.

title: Purpose of Code of Ethics
summary: Helps identify ethical dilemmas, holds social workers accountable, and provides standards for evaluating conduct.

title: Commitment to Clients (1.01)
summary: Primary responsibility is promoting client well-being. Safety overrides confidentiality when risk is present.

title: Self-Determination (1.02)
summary: Clients have the right to make their own choices unless they pose imminent risk to themselves or others.

title: Informed Consent (1.03)
summary: Clients must understand services, risks, costs, confidentiality limits, and have the right to refuse.

title: Confidentiality
summary: Information is private unless there is risk of harm, abuse, or legal requirement to disclose.
